lines.text: images stunned with surprise! & the dream it se
that I was a child again watching grandfather at
work bench, and tho' at the time of that strange drea
grandfather was then deceased, but as I seemed to be
standing beside him in the dream, he looked down at
and said "Florence whatever I dream always comes tru
Actually I had never, while he lived with us in all those
ever heard him claim he had dreamed; so my only inte
ation of the dream & what he said in it would mean that
what I dreamed etc. when he was young he was ord
"goose-step" into Prussia in the Franco-Prussian war
He often told me in German how he hated war, & I know
how much he loved the beautiful & how really kind
was. He loved garden, flowers & did beautiful cabinet
Doesn't it stand to reason that there is a very def
purpose in the disintregation of the "saucers" now?
That we have advertised the fact that we think its
are "interplanetary", and the country sending them are
affraid that we may find out different? That also there
be chemicals & desease germs within them that if they ret
to the original base might accidently be scattered withi
their own boundaries and dangerous to their own welfare
And besides after all we have found out that m
is not inhabited! I might add now that I did not sig
my name to the letters I wrote about that dream to the thr
Washington gentlemen, I was afraid to for we were so clo
to war with Germany then. I mailed them from Tulsa, so of
might have heard of this happening at that time. We know
that dream of 3 nights did not then come true. But th
is still time that something very similar may yet happ
Enclosed is postage for mailing this to "the par
you know will consider it from a serious possibility an
also a rough sketch that I've made, my idea of Russia's
method. I would appreciate hearing your opinio
on the above scientific possibility mentioned. I know noth
of Science actually. I am Sincerely yours.
Wish I did.
Mrs [REDACTED]
